status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 8 / REFERENCE-SEQUENCE OR IDENTITY-CONFLICT CONTROL ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named cartilage / connective-tissue short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: AED TRIPEPTIDE / CARTILAGE POSITIONING AND SUPPLIER EQUIVALENCE
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — NLM MESH/PUBCHEM IDENTITY ALA-GLU-ASP (AED) AND PRECLINICAL CELLULAR LITERATURE IDENTIFIED; LAX20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND CONTROLLED HUMAN BENEFIT OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: NLM MeSH and PubChem identify Cartalax as Ala-Glu-Asp (AED). Published work is predominantly cell, stem-cell, organotypic and ageing-model research. It does not establish cartilage regeneration, osteoarthritis treatment or LAX20 analytical and clinical equivalence.
critical_closure_controls: AED database identity is not LAX20 analytical proof
Do not confuse Cartalax AED with Cortagen AEDP
Cell and connective-tissue models are not human cartilage repair
No arthritis, joint, cartilage-regeneration or anti-ageing claim
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
